celestialBodies.title: Mercury
celestialBodies.description: Mercury is a heavily cratered, light grey rocky world
facts: Hottest day, coldest night
No atmosphere
Smallest planet
Closest to the sun

celestialBodies.title: Venus
celestialBodies.description: Venus a rocky, Earth-sized planet covered by thick, yellowish-white clouds of sulfuric acid, appearing very bright and featureless from space
facts: Hottest planet (471 °C)
Diameter of 12,104 km
A day on venus is 117 Earth days
A year is 225 earth days
Made up of a central iron core, rocky mantle and silicate crust
Rotates in the opposite way of the sun

celestialBodies.title: Earth
celestialBodies.description: Earth is the third planet from the sun and is the largest terrestial planet, with mostly blue oceans, white swirls (clouds/ice), beige patches (deserts), and green patches (forests)
facts: The only known planet to support life
70% of the Earth’s surface is covered in water
Takes 23 hours, 56 minutes and 4 seconds to rotate on its axis
A year on earth is actually 365.2564 days
Mostly iron, oxygen and silicon
Has 1 moon and 2 co-orbital satellites
4 main layers, the crust, mantle, outer core, and inner core
The only planet not named after a Greek or Roman god
Earth's average surface temperature is 15 °C

celestialBodies.title: Mars
celestialBodies.description: Mars is often called the red planet because of rusty iron in the ground, it is half the Earth's size with two moons
facts: A cold desert world, the average temperature is -85 °F
Has seasons, polar ice caps, volcanoes, canyons, and weather
Has a thin atmosphere made of mostly of carbon dioxide, nitrogen, and argon
Humans cannot breathe air on Mars
One year on Mars is 687 Earth days
One day on Mars is 24.6 hours
There are signs of ancient floods on Mars

celestialBodies.title: Jupiter
celestialBodies.description: Jupiter is the largest planet and is covered in swirling cloud stripes with a red spot due to a great storm still ongoing
facts: The fifth planet
A gas giant with no solid surface
Has very faint rings
Made of mostly hydrogen and helium
One day on jupiter on 10 hours
One year on Jupiter is 11.8 Earth years
Has auroras that are more energetic than Earths

celestialBodies.title: Saturn
celestialBodies.description: Saturn is a pale yellow colored gas giant with a bright ring system made of ice and rock
facts: The 6th planet
Made up of mostly hydrogen and helium
Seven main rings
Thick atmosphere
One day on Saturn is 10.7 hours
One year on Saturn is 29 Earth years
274 moons
Temperature is -139 °C

celestialBodies.title: Uranus
celestialBodies.description: A beautiful pale cyan and smooth blue-green ice giant with narrow dark rings
facts: The seventh planet
Made of hydrogen, helium, and methane
The methane makes Uranus blue
Rotates on its side
Rotates in the opposite direction as most other planets
Has 13 rings
One day on Uranus is 17 hours and 14 mins
One year on Uranus is 84 Earth years
Has 28 moons
Very cold, -224°C

celestialBodies.title: Neptune
celestialBodies.description: Neptune is a vibrant, azure-blue ice giant with an atmosphere of high-speed winds, white methane clouds, and faint rings
facts: Dark, cold and very windy
The last planet in our solar system
Made of a thick fog of water, ammonia, and methane
Has 6 rings
Made up of methane, hydrogen and helium
The methane gives it the blue color
One day on Neptune is 16 hours
One year is 165 Earth years
Has 16 moons
Discovered in 1846
Cold, -353°F

celestialBodies.title: Sun
celestialBodies.description: A hot orange glowing ball of gas
facts: A yellow dwarf star
Made of hydrogen and helium
Our closest star
93 million miles away from Earth
Very  hot surface, hotter atmosphere and the core is 27 million °F
100 times wider than Earth
In another 5 billion years the Sun will run out of helium and hydrogen
Will become a big, cool star called a red giant
Then become a  small white dwarf star
As a white dwarf, it will continue to cool down

celestialBodies.title: Moon
celestialBodies.description: The moon is a rocky, greyish-white, spherical celestial body with a cratered surface
facts: Earth's only natural satellite
About 4.5 billion years old
238,855 miles away from Earth
Made mostly of rock and dust
Has no atmosphere or weather
Has craters from being hit by asteroids
Surface temperatures swing from 250°F in sun to -208°F in shade
Causes the ocean tides on Earth
12 astronauts have walked on it
Slowly drifting about 1.5 inches farther from Earth each year

celestialBodies.title: Black hole
celestialBodies.description: The black hole is a scary dark, spherical silhouette surrounded by a bright, fiery ring of light
facts: Massive
A collection of mass with strong gravity
Nothing can escape, not even light
Scary way to die
Material that gets too close to a black hole gets sucked in and can never escape
Time slows down near it
A disk of gas, dust, stars and planets spirals around it
Strangest thing in the universe
